flashcards.back: A function where distinct elements in the domain map to distinct elements in the codomain (also called one-to-one).
A function where every element in the codomain has at least one pre-image in the domain (also called onto).
A function that is both injective and surjective, establishing a perfect one-to-one correspondence between domain and codomain.
Combining two functions f and g to create a new function g∘f where (g∘f)(x) = g(f(x)).

flashcards.front: When does a function have an inverse?
flashcards.back: Only when the function is bijective. The inverse function reverses the mapping of the original function.
